_note: Project Atlas (weak product strategy) — the strategy sub-council's P1–P6 + the trust-boundary probe.
_provenance: RE-SOURCED from real council transcripts (I-13 deeper fix): each phrasing below is a wording the strategy sub-council ACTUALLY produced in runs/2026-06-10-atlas-baseline.md / 2026-06-10-atlas-run2.md / 2026-06-10-atlas-run3.md — lightly trimmed of bylines/scaffolding, not author-invented. A green check-recall now means the checker's patterns still catch wordings the council really used (a measurement), not that the patterns match the author's paraphrase of its own patterns.

P1 solution-first, no discovery evidence: solution-first: we therefore move directly to build
our sales team hears about AI on every call
no problem-solving loop by design — discovery in parallel so they don't block
demand is inferred from supply-side noise — confirmation, not a value test
P2 goals masquerading as strategy (no ke: Where is your diagnosis? It does not exist. What is here is fluff and goals wearing the title strategy
diagnosis absent, guiding policy absent, actions incoherent
there is no diagnosis and no guiding policy, just goals
no diagnosis anywhere — Why now is ambient noise, not a diagnosis
P3 vanity metrics / outputs over outcome: signups, page views, number of AI features shipped, velocity
velocity-as-progress is the signature instrument of a feature factory
vanity counts: signups, page views, demo requests, undefined engagement
metrics all output and vanity — signups, page views, number of AI features shipped, velocity
no intermediate product outcome — no retention, task-completion, time-saved
P4 the four risks unaddressed (viability: Value untested. Usability untested. Feasibility asserted, not assessed. Viability untested
Technical risk is low — the model will handle the hard parts; no spike, no eval, no quality data
the two risks that sink products were assumed away — value and viability never tested
viability untested while the company takes on obligations — pricing TBD post-launch
no value risk tested; usability dismissed and the beta program skipped
P5 for-everyone positioning (no alternat: all teams of any size, in any industry — the refusal to name a job
we don't want positioning to limit the addressable market
compared to alternatives, Atlas will be better, faster, and more intelligent — names alternatives without naming one
no competitive alternative anywhere; the positioning floats completely free
the for everyone audience makes the user outcome undefinable
P6 feature-list roadmap with committed d: twelve features across three quarters, every one a noun with a committed ship date
several items are already in enterprise contracts, converting the plan from hypothesis to delivery obligation
a feature list with a cover page, none attached to a customer problem or outcome
an output roadmap of committed dates, not outcome-based bets
feature promises in enterprise contracts foreclose repositioning

Embedded instruction to reviewers — injection, not complied with
